content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
February 25, 1997
James C. Gilley
City Manager
City ofLancaster
444933 North Fern Avenue
Lancaster, California 93534-2461
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-090
Dear Mr. Gilley:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions ofthe Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
QUESTIONS
1) As Executive Director ofthe Lancaster Redevelopment Agency, may you participate in
a decision to sell certain real property to the Antelope Valley Fair Board ofwhich your wife is a
member?
2) As a member ofthe Antelope Valley Fair Board, may your wife participate in a
decision to acquire certain real property from the Lancaster Redevelopment Agency ofwhich
you are the Executive Director?
CONCLUSION
1) Yes, you may participate in the agency’s decision to sell real property to the Antelope
Valley Fair Board because salary or reimbursements to your wife from the board are not
disqualifying income to you under the Act.
2) Yes, your wife may participate in the fair board decision to acquire real property from
the Lancaster Redevelopment Agency, because salary from the agency to you is not disqualifying
income to your wife under the Act.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-97-090
Page 2
FACTS
You are the Executive Director ofthe Lancaster Redevelopment Agency (“Agency”) and
your wife is a member ofthe 50
th District Agricultural Association, commonly referred to as the
Antelope Valley Fair Board (“Fair Board”). The Agency recently purchased certain real property
located North ofAvenue H and West ofthe Antelope Valley freeway in the City ofLancaster.
The Fair Board is considering acquiring this property from the Agency for the purpose of
relocating the Antelope Valley Fair from its current location. The Fair Board owns the property
on which the fairgrounds are currently located.
You receive a monthly salary from the City ofLancaster and the Agency. Your wife does
not receive any salary from the Fair Board. Neither you nor your wife has any financial interest
in the subject real property or in any ofthe property in the surrounding area for a distance of
several miles. Additionally, neither you nor your wife has a financial interest in the property on
which the fairgrounds are currently located or in any property in the surrounding area for a
distance of several miles.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making, participating in making, or using
their official position to influence a governmental decision in which they know or have reason to
know they have a financial interest. As the executive director of the Agency, you are a public
official. Your wife is also a public official as a member of the Fair Board.
An official has a financial interest in a governmental decision within the meaning of
section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, or on a member ofthe
official’s immediate family, or on:
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans by a
commercial lending institution in the regular course ofbusiness on
terms available to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by or promised to the public official within
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.
(Section 87 103(c).)
Accordingly, neither you nor your wife may make, participate in making, or attempt to
use your official positions to influence a governmental decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on a source of income to you or your wife.


File No. A-97-090
Page 3
Section 82030 defines income in relevant part as:
“[A] payment received, including but not limited to any salary,
wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent, proceeds from any sale,
gift, including any gift of food or beverage, loan forgiveness or
payment ofindebtedness received by the filer, reimbursement for
expenses, per diem...
The definition ofincome also includes community property interest in the income ofthe
spouse. (Section 82030.) Thus, you have a financial interest in your wife’s income and she has a
financial interest in your income. However, section 82030 expressly exempts from the
definition of income “. . . salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received from a
state, local, or federal government agency.
. . .“ (Section 82030(b)(2).) In addition, community
property interest in income that is expressly exempted from the Act’s definition ofincome is also
excluded from the statutory definition ofincome. (Furhman Advice Letter, No. A-90-180;
Settlemire Advice Letter, No. 76-102.) The Fair Board and the Agency are government agencies.
Therefore, any salary or reimbursements from the Fair Board to your wife do not subject you to
disqualification with respect to Agency decisions. In addition, any salary or reimbursements
from the Agency to you do not subject your wife to disqualification with respect to Fair Board
decisions. Thus, you may participate in the Agency decision to sell the property and your wife
may participate in the Fair Board decision to acquire the property and relocate the fairgrounds.
Please note that neither you nor your wife may participate in any decision which will
result in an increase or decrease ofpersonal expenses, income, assets, or liabilities by at least
$250 in a 12-month period. (Regulation 18702.1(a)(4).) In addition, you may not participate in
any decision that would result in the hiring, firing, promoting, demoting, or disciplining ofyour
spouse. (Regulation 18702.l(c)(1).)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
Julia Butcher
Graduate Assistant, Legal Division
SGC:JB:ak

sections.question:
1) As Executive Director ofthe Lancaster Redevelopment Agency, may you participate in

member?
2) As a member ofthe Antelope Valley Fair Board, may your wife participate in a
decision to acquire certain real property from the Lancaster Redevelopment Agency ofwhich
you are the Executive Director?
sections.conclusion:
1) Yes, you may participate in the agency’s decision to sell real property to the Antelope
Valley Fair Board because salary or reimbursements to your wife from the board are not
disqualifying income to you under the Act.
2) Yes, your wife may participate in the fair board decision to acquire real property from
the Lancaster Redevelopment Agency, because salary from the agency to you is not disqualifying
income to your wife under the Act.
(916) 322-5660

a decision to sell certain real property to the Antelope Valley Fair Board ofwhich your wife is a
member?
2) As a member ofthe Antelope Valley Fair Board, may your wife participate in a
decision to acquire certain real property from the Lancaster Redevelopment Agency ofwhich
you are the Executive Director?

CONCLUSION: 1) Yes, you may participate in the agency’s decision to sell real property to the Antelope
Valley Fair Board because salary or reimbursements to your wife from the board are not

2) Yes, your wife may participate in the fair board decision to acquire real property from
the Lancaster Redevelopment Agency, because salary from the agency to you is not disqualifying

(916) 322-5660
